name: Sprint 10

team.name: team-lead
team.owns: arbitration, src/template content, ADRs, final approval

team.name: scribe
team.owns: work-tracking JSON, CONTEXT.md terms, Retro Loop

goal: Harden to-execution issue create flag parsing so agents stop hitting silent data corruption and misleading errors.

shipped.title: LIST flags append per occurrence and stop comma-splitting prose
shipped.summary: parseFlags now contributes exactly one verbatim trimmed item per LIST-flag occurrence and appends on repeat; comma-splitting and LIST_VALUE_SEPARATOR are retired and the write-policy comment corrected. Affects --scope, --acceptance-criteria, --depends-on, --related-tickets. Closes the silent-corruption path where a prose criterion full of commas collapsed into N items with no error.

shipped.title: Presence flags reject a trailing value with a legible error
shipped.summary: A BOOLEAN/NULL presence flag handed a trailing non-flag token (e.g. --actionable-now true) now throws a by-name error naming the flag as a presence flag to pass alone, instead of the misleading 'unknown flag true'. A bare presence flag still sets the field; a known flag following one still parses normally.

shipped.title: issue create and issue update print a --help flag reference
shipped.summary: issue create --help and issue update --help (and -h) render the ISSUE_FIELD_FLAGS reference from FLAG_TYPE_LABEL — each flag with its type label (STRING / LIST-repeatable / BOOLEAN / INTEGER) and a note that list flags repeat one item per occurrence. Depends on S10-1 so the labels and the repeat note describe the shipped behavior.

shipped.title: Refresh execution-to-issues skill to teach the issue create CLI
shipped.summary: Rewrote the execution-to-issues SKILL.md write step from the pre-ADR-0011 backlog.json append flow to the real to-execution issue create recipe: one create per slice, minted UUIDv7 ids, repeated list flags for multi-item fields, bare presence flags, and dependency-order id capture for --depends-on. Dropped the scan-for-max-EXEC-NNN id guidance. Depends on S10-1 so the recipe shows the shipped flag behavior.

decisions.title: List flags are repeatable; comma-splitting removed
decisions.summary: A LIST flag contributes one verbatim item per occurrence and repeats append; comma-splitting and LIST_VALUE_SEPARATOR were retired. Chosen over switching to a different separator character because no single character is prose-safe. The change is mildly breaking, but the blast radius is one documented caller and ephemeral records — acceptable pre-1.0.

decisions.title: parseFlags exported and main() guarded for unit-testing
decisions.summary: parseFlags was exported and main() guarded with require.main === module, so requiring cli.js exposes the parser without firing the CLI and the parsing behavior is unit-testable in isolation.

retrospective_notes.title: Filing these issues tripped the very bug they fix
retrospective_notes.body: Filing the Sprint 10 issues hit the comma-split bug first-hand: an --acceptance-criteria value full of prose commas collapsed into one blob item. That is direct evidence the fix was needed, and it is captured in S10-1's acceptance criteria. All four slices were chartered (planned slices, not emergent), so the Retro Loop needs no alignment gate and no teammate-definition edits — the observation is recorded here only.

step_log.artifact: S10-1/S10-2/S10-3 issue-flag hardening: src/bin/cli.js (parseFlags LIST repeat-append, presence-flag misuse error, issue create|update --help, parseFlags export + require.main guard), src/bin/write-policy.js (FLAG_TYPE_LABEL, retire LIST_VALUE_SEPARATOR), src/test/write-record.test.js
step_log.summary: PASS — 0 violations on re-gate. Named constants (HELP_FLAGS, FLAG_TYPE_LABEL), data/logic split (labels live in write-policy.js), and fail-closed error paths all hold.

PASS — 0 violations. require.main===module guard confirmed (requiring cli.js returns { parseFlags } without firing main()); full stamp contract intact; 31/31 node:test tests green; src/package.json untouched.
